Question: Evaluate $$\int_{-3}^{+ \infty} e^{\text{unicode[:-]} x} d x .$$ State whether the improper integral converges or diverges.
Evaluate $$\int_{0}^{2} \frac{1}{x} d x .$$ State whether the integral converges or diverges.
Use a comparison to show that $$\int_{e}^{+ \infty} \frac{\text{ln} x}{x} d x$$ diverges.
Answer: Since $$\int_{e}^{+ \infty} \frac{1}{x} d x = \text{+} \infty ,$$ $$\int_{e}^{+ \infty} \frac{\text{ln} x}{x} d x$$ diverges.
